title: Superadmin Tenant Management

story.i_want_to: list, view, update, suspend, and activate tenants via superadmin API endpoints
story.so_that: I can manage the multi-tenant platform, handle abuse or billing issues by suspending tenants, and reactivate them when resolved

rationale: A multi-tenant platform needs administrative controls that operate outside any single tenant's scope. Superadmin endpoints provide the ability to view all tenants, modify their settings or status, and suspend/activate tenants as needed. These operations use a superadmin API key (tenant_id=NULL) that bypasses normal RLS scoping to operate across the entire system.

acceptance_criteria.description: GET /api/v1/admin/tenants returns a paginated list of all tenants with summary stats: id, name, slug, email, status, project_count, story_count, agent_count, api_key_count, inserted_at. Requires superadmin API key.
GET /api/v1/admin/tenants/:id returns full tenant detail including settings, all summary stats, and recent activity (last API call timestamp).
PATCH /api/v1/admin/tenants/:id allows updating tenant fields: name, email, settings (partial merge — provided keys override, unspecified keys preserved), status. Returns updated tenant.
POST /api/v1/admin/tenants/:id/suspend sets tenant status to 'suspended'. Suspended tenants' API keys return 403 on all requests (checked in auth pipeline). Returns updated tenant.
POST /api/v1/admin/tenants/:id/activate sets tenant status to 'active'. Previously suspended tenants regain full access. Returns updated tenant.
All admin/tenants endpoints require a superadmin API key (role=superadmin, tenant_id=NULL). Non-superadmin keys receive 403.
Suspend and activate actions are recorded in the audit log with actor_type='superadmin', the superadmin's API key ID as actor_id, and the tenant_id of the affected tenant.
The tenants list supports filtering by status (active, suspended, deactivated) and search by name or slug (case-insensitive partial match).
Attempting to suspend an already-suspended tenant returns 422 with a descriptive error. Same for activating an already-active tenant.
The auth pipeline checks tenant status after resolving the API key. If the tenant is suspended, return 403 with error message 'Tenant suspended' before processing any request.

test_cases.name: list tenants with stats
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
Returns both tenants
Tenant A includes project_count=1, agent_count=1
Tenant B includes project_count=0, agent_count=0
Each tenant includes id, name, slug, email, status, inserted_at

test_cases.name: get tenant detail with full stats
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
Includes full tenant detail with settings
Includes project_count, story_count, epic_count, agent_count, api_key_count

test_cases.name: suspend tenant blocks their API access
steps: GET /api/v1/tenants/me with the suspended tenant's user API key
expected_results: Suspend response is 200 OK with tenant status='suspended'
Tenant's own API call returns 403 with error 'Tenant suspended'
Audit log entry exists with action='tenant_suspended'

test_cases.name: activate tenant restores their API access
steps: GET /api/v1/tenants/me with the reactivated tenant's user API key
expected_results: Activate response is 200 OK with tenant status='active'
Tenant's own API call returns 200 OK (access restored)
Audit log entry exists with action='tenant_activated'

test_cases.name: non-superadmin cannot access admin endpoints
expected_results: Both requests return 403 Forbidden

test_cases.name: update tenant settings with partial merge
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
Tenant settings.max_webhooks is 20
Tenant settings.max_projects is still 50 (preserved, not overwritten)

test_cases.name: filter tenants by status
expected_results: Response includes only the suspended tenant
Active tenant is not in the results

test_cases.name: suspending already-suspended tenant returns 422
expected_results: Response is 422 Unprocessable Entity
Error message indicates tenant is already suspended

technical_notes: Create AdminTenantController with index, show, update, suspend, activate actions.
Routes: scope /api/v1/admin, pipe_through [:api, :require_superadmin]. All routes under /admin require superadmin role.
Superadmin API keys have tenant_id=NULL. The auth pipeline must handle this case: bypass RLS (or set a special RLS context that grants cross-tenant access).
For tenant stats, use subqueries or lateral joins: SELECT t.*, (SELECT count(*) FROM projects WHERE tenant_id = t.id) as project_count, etc.
Tenant suspension check: add a plug in the auth pipeline after ResolveApiKey that checks tenant.status. If suspended, halt with 403.
Settings merge on PATCH: Map.merge(existing_settings, provided_settings). Deep merge if needed for nested settings.
Audit logging for suspend/activate: actor_type='superadmin', tenant_id=affected_tenant.id (not NULL, since the action is about that tenant).
Search filter: WHERE lower(name) LIKE lower('%query%') OR lower(slug) LIKE lower('%query%').
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
All context functions MUST return `{:ok, result}` or `{:error, reason}` tuples. Never raise exceptions for expected error cases.
Controller MUST use `action_fallback LoopctlWeb.FallbackController` for error rendering (maps error atoms to HTTP status codes per US-1.4)
